Stellaris Wiki

Jobs

Version

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article. At least some were last verified for version 4.3.

This article is for the PC version of Stellaris only.

Planetary management

Planetary management
Designation
Districts
District specialization
Planet capital
Buildings
Holdings
Jobs

A Job is where resources are produced on worlds. Jobs are divided into different strata and higher stratum jobs are usually filled before lower stratum ones. Most Jobs are created by Districts and Buildings and thus limited in number. Depending on its origin an empire will use one of two job systems:

  • Jobs in empires that do not have the Wilderness origin are worked by pops and receive efficiency bonuses and penalties depending on the traits of the pop working the job. Pops automatically fill empty jobs that they are capable of holding and generally choose the highest stratum job that gets the most out of their traits based on a weighting system; however, a priority can be set towards and away from specific jobs to ensure they are filled before others. New pops that are more suitable for a job than the current pop holding the job may take the job from it. Each job has a slider that can be lowered to decrease the maximum number of pops that can have the job.
  • Jobs in empires that have the Wilderness origin are worked without pops and receive efficiency bonuses and penalties depending on the traits of the primary species. Each job has a slider that can be lowered to decrease the resource consumption and production of the job.

Job categories

Most jobs belong to a job category that is mentioned on certain tooltips, and groups it together with other similar jobs. This allows modifiers to apply to multiple job types.

For instance, Alloy Mega-Forges increasing output and upkeep by +1 Alloys and −1 Minerals from Metallurgists will also provide additional output and upkeep to Odd Factory Workers.

All sub-categories will always inherit multiplicative modifiers from their parent category, but only a few specified sub-categories also inherit additive modifiers.

Stratum indicates the super-category these categories are included into, which might differ from the actual stratum these pops occupy. For instance, even though Lord Commanders are placed in the Elite stratum, they will not benefit from Elite resource output modifiers (even if they benefit from other Elite affecting bonuses), and will instead be affected by Specialist resource output modifiers.

Stratum Category Sub-category Jobs Event jobs Base output and upkeep modifiers

    • All Politician job swaps

The following jobs inherit modifiers from other categories, even if they are not classified under said category:

Job Inherits from

Grid Amalgamated Technicians

Job swaps

Some jobs can change to alternate versions when certain conditions are met. The swapped jobs can produce additional or different resources, but otherwise inherit the same modifiers as the base job.

For instance, Politician jobs are replaced by Noble jobs if the empire has the Aristocratic Elite civic. The new Noble jobs adds +0.5 Stability per 100 workforce, in addition to the resources produced by regular Politician jobs.

If conditions are fulfilled for more than one swap, the swap with the highest weight will take precedence. However, save exceptions, the output changes will stack. For instance, a Technophant in an empire with the Exalted Priesthood civic will produce both +2 Engineering and +1 Unity per 100 workforce.

Elite jobs

Elite stratum jobs. Gestalt Consciousness empires do not use this stratum or its jobs, nor use any equivalent. Worker Cooperative uses Bureaucrat or equivalent jobs instead.

Job Cat. Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Primary source Weight

      • Tier III temple / bureaucratic buildings

  • ×0.1 if not Full Citizenship rights

Specialist and Complex Drone jobs

Specialist and Complex Drone stratum jobs.

Pops under Chattel Slavery cannot work Specialist or Elite jobs. Other slave types may work some Specialist jobs, see species rights for details.

Industrial jobs

Industrial jobs produce advanced resources, typically using minerals upkeep.

Metallurgists and Artisans also produce strategic resources if a Chemical Plant, Exotic Gas Refinery or Synthetic Crystal Plant, respectively, is present, at the cost of Minerals upkeep ( Food with
Catalytic Processing
Catalytic Processing
Catalytic Recyclers
Organic Reprocessing
Organic Retrofitting
civic).

  • Heavy Industry zones
  • Alloy Foundries

Research jobs

Research jobs produce research, typically using consumer goods upkeep. Gestalt job variants use minerals or energy upkeep instead.

Job Cat. Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Primary source Empire Weight

Unity jobs produce unity, typically using consumer goods upkeep. Gestalt job variants use energy upkeep instead.

Pops employed in Priest category jobs have ×0.1 Materialist ethic attraction.

Transference Volunteer
Transference Drone

  • +1% Ship armor
 −5 Food
  • Behemoth Fury ascension perk:
    • Mindlink Metropolis
 Any Base: 20
Modifiers:
  • × pop output
  • ×2 if Resilient
  • ×0.2 if not Full Citizenship rights
  • ×0.1 if can work Servant job
  • ×0.1 if Indentured Servitude

Death Chroniclers and Civilians (the latter when a Monument is on planet) gain additional effects depending on the empire's governing ethics:

Ethic Regular effect per 100 workforce Fanatic effect per 100 workforce
Authoritarian +1 Edict fund +2 Edict fund
Egalitarian +0.5 Trade +1 Trade
Materialist +0.1 Research +0.2 Research
Spiritualist +0.25 Unity +0.5 Unity
Militarist +0.125 Country naval capacity +0.25 Country naval capacity
Pacifist +100 Planet amenities +200 Planet amenities
Xenophobe +0.625% Planet citizen happiness +1.25% Planet citizen happiness
Xenophile −0.625% Planet pop upkeep −1.25% Planet pop upkeep

Worker and Menial Drone jobs

Worker and Menial Drone jobs.

Tankbound pops can't work jobs in this stratum.

Slave jobs can only be worked by pops belonging to a species that is enslaved.

Job Cat. Stratum Output and upkeep Pop modifiers Slavery type Source Empire Description
Servant —

 −0.5 Housing usage if organic

Servants perform various menial domestic tasks and household work for their masters.
Livestock

 Livestock Automatically generated for all pops

  • +100 Amenities

  • +100 Amenities

 Livestock

These xenos are pitted in combat against our warriors, entertaining the masses while keeping the bestial population at sustainable levels.
Overseer
  • −25 Crime
  • +1 Planetary defense armies
 Any

    • Governor's Palace / Estates

These relatively privileged slaves keep their less fortunate brethren in line.

  • +4 Naval capacity

  • −1 Alloys

  • Thrall-World:

Elite slave warriors, trained from birth, who fight with unparalleled zeal and ferocity. Their loyalty and dedication make them formidable on the front lines.
Toiler —
  • +200 Amenities
 Any
  • Thrall-World:
    • Automatically generated for unemployed pops
Toilers perform the grueling menial labor required to keep the crude infrastructure of the Thrall-World running.
Grid Amalgamated +4 Energy −0.5 Housing usage Grid Amalgamation Automatically generated for all pops
Their organic energy will be absorbed, until they expire.
Organic Specimen
  • +0.5 Unity
  • −1 Food
 −0.5 Housing usage Sapient Specimens Automatically generated for all pops
The uplifted organics enhance our drones' life-creation subroutines, infusing new perspectives and lessons into future projects.
Orderly —
  • +500 Amenities
  • +2.5% Experimental Engineer and Test Subject job efficiency
  • Orderlies
 Automatically generated for all pops Any
These workers are responsible for the day-to-day care of those subjected to experimental procedures that often render them unable to care for themselves.

Special jobs

Civilian / Maintenance Drones

Unemployed pops will automatically demote to Civilian or Maintenance Drone stratum.

Job Cat. Output per 100 workforce Primary source Empire Weight
Civilian —
  • +50 Amenities
  • +1 Trade
 Generated for all unemployed pops —
Hedonist —
  • +1 Trade
  • Civilian job swap:
    • Pleasure Seekers
      Pleasure Seekers
      Corporate Hedonism
      civic
 Job swap: 5
Tourist —
  • +2 Trade
  • Civilian job swap:
    • Resort World
 Job swap: 6
Maintenance Drone —
  • +400 Amenities
  • +1 Trade
 Generated for all unemployed pops —

Undergoing Assimilation

Pops set to Assimilation citizenship have their stratum changed to Assimilation and are automatically set to Undergoing Assimilation jobs. The job's effects depend on the assimilation type.

Purging

Pops set to Undesirables citizenship have their stratum changed to Undesirables and are automatically set to Purging jobs that produce resources based on the purge type. Certain purge types are inherit base output modifiers from certain categories.

Purged pops also produce additional resources for certain empire types, regardless of purge type:

  • With Fanatic Purifiers:
    • +1 Monthly unity per 100 purging pops
    • 5~100 Unity
      This amount is equal to 1.5x your monthly gross Unity income, with a minimum of 5 and a maximum of 100.
      per 100 purged pops
  • With Devouring Swarm:
    • +1 Monthly society research per 100 purging pops
    • 1~100 Society Research
      This amount is equal to your monthly gross Society Research income, with a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 100.
      per 100 purged pops
    • If Wilderness, gain Biomass equal to 10% of purged pops
Purge type Happiness Monthly output per 100 pops Effects on death per 100 pops Inherits modifiers from
Displacement −30%
Neutering −20%
Necrophage −50% Purged pops become founder species[1]
Extermination −1000%
5~100 Unity
This amount is equal to 2x your monthly gross Unity income, with a minimum of 5 and a maximum of 100.
(if and pop is biological)
Forced Labor −1000%
  • +3 Food
  • +3 Minerals
+24 Food
+16 Minerals
+12 Alloys
+12 Alloys
  • Farmers
  • Miners
Processing −1000%
  • +6 Food
    +6 Minerals
    +2 Alloys
    +3 Alloys
  • +12 Food
  • +12 Minerals
Farmers
Miners
Metallurgists
Chemical Processing −1000% +6 Energy +24 Energy Technicians
Material −1000% +5 Consumer Goods Artisans

Subversive jobs

High crime, deviancy, or unemployment can create planet modifiers that add subversive jobs. Pops will abandon subversive jobs if Elite, Specialist, or Soldier jobs become available. All subversive jobs have negative output.
Experimental Sentencing
Experimental Sentencing
Judicial R&D
Corrective Consciousness
empires can construct Experimentation Chambers that make these jobs productive.
Job Output per 100 workforce Requirements Description
Criminal
  • +7.5 Criminal branch office Trade
 Criminal-related planet modifier
These criminals live outside the bounds of legal society, some by choice and some by circumstances.
Test Subject
  • +7.5 Criminal branch office Trade
  • +0.5 Physics
  • +1 Society
  • +0.5 Engineering
  • +300 Amenities
  • −1 Consumer Goods
 Experimentation Chambers
Convicted of criminal activity, these individuals have been put to productive use as live test subjects in technological experiments.
Deviant Drone −1 Energy Drone Deviancy planet modifier
These deviants no longer obey the Hive Mind.
Test Subject Drone
  • +0.5 Physics
  • +1 Society
  • +0.5 Engineering
  • +300 Amenities
  • −1 Energy
 Experimentation Chambers
After straying from the will of the collective, these deviants have been put to productive use as live test subjects in technological experiments.
Corrupt Drone −1 Energy Drone Corruption planet modifier
These drones have been corrupted and no longer obey the directives of the core intelligence.

Pre-sapient jobs

Pre-sapients are not normally productive, but depending on the Pre-Sapients policy, they can produce amenities or resources. Pre-sapient pops use the Pre-Sapients stratum.

Job Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Source
Pre-Sapient — — Default for pre-sapients
Hunted Pre-Sapient
  • : +4 Food
    • +1 / 2 Food with Food Processing Facilities
  • : +2 Minerals
    • +1 / 2 Minerals with Mineral Purification Plants
 — Pre-Sapients policy is Hunted
Conserved Fauna +100 Amenities
  • : −1 Food
  • : −1 Minerals
  • Pre-Sapients policy is Protected
  • Alien Zoo
  • Warrior Culture
Gladiatorial Beast +100 Amenities
  • : −1 Food
  • : −1 Minerals
  • Pre-Sapients policy is Protected
  • Alien Zoo
  • Warrior Culture

Bio-Trophy

Organic pops in Rogue Servitor empires use a special Bio-Trophy stratum and jobs. Employed Bio-Trophy pops have their housing needs automatically fulfilled.

Job Cat. Output per 100 workforce Sources
Bio-Trophy
  • +1 Unity
  • +1% Complex Drone output
  • −1 Housing usage for this pop
  • Organic Sanctuaries
  • Sanctuary Arcologies

Neural Chip

Pops on the Synaptic Lathe have their stratum changed to Computing Component and are automatically set to Neural Chip jobs.

Job Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Description
Neural Chip
  • Multiplied by total Neural Chips / 2,000
  • +0.375 Society
  • +0.375 Physics
  • +0.375 Engineering
  • +0.025 Advanced Logic
  • Multiplied by total Neural Chips / 4,000
  • −4.5
A single Neural Chip is inconsequential. Once connected, their processing capabilities increase exponentially.

Origin jobs

Unique jobs unlocked by certain origins.

Broken Shackles jobs

Available only with the First Contact DLC enabled.

Empires with the Broken Shackles origin start with unique buildings that provide low tech jobs. They are replaced by regular jobs once the buildings are upgraded.

Job Cat. Stratum Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Source Description
Administrator Specialist +2 Unity −1 Consumer goods Administrative Hub (if )
Our former masters delegated much of their empire's day-to-day administration to indentured assets. The result was a class of highly trained logistics personnel.
Supervisor Specialist
  • +2 Unity
  • +2 Trade
 −1 Consumer goods Administrative Hub (if Corporate)
Our former masters delegated much of their empire's day-to-day administration to indentured assets. The result was a class of highly trained managers.
Preacher Specialist
  • +2 Unity
  • +200 Amenities
 −1 Consumer goods Administrative Hub (if Spiritualist)
Our preachers remind us that freedom is a divine right; united in faith, we find our common strength.
Scientist Specialist +1 Research −1 Consumer goods Laboratory Complex
Our former masters ignored our gifts and downplayed our intelligence. Now, the keenest minds among us are free to follow their ambition.
Militia — Specialist
  • −10 Crime
  • +1 Planetary defense army
 — Command Center
Indentured soldiers were mainly used as cannon fodder; the fortunate few who survived have volunteered to protect our newfound freedom with their lives.
Laborer Worker
  • +3 Consumer goods
  • +1.5 Alloys
 — Scrap Refinery
As assets they were worked to the bone. With freedom has come renewed purpose, and a vigor that no slave could ever hope to match.
Prospector Worker +3 Minerals — Scrap Refinery
Stubborn, ill-mannered, indomitable; these former indentured miners are as solid as scrap metal.
Reactor Engineer Worker +4 Energy — Power Plant
Aboard their masters' vessels, indentured engineers were often assigned the lowliest and most dangerous jobs. This hardship only sharpened their skills.
Homesteader Worker +4 Food — Makeshift Farms
The agri-worlds of our former masters were scenes of brutal oppression. Now, our homesteaders are free to enjoy the fruits of their labor.
Scavenger Worker
  • +1 Alloys
  • +0.5 Research
  • +200 Amenities
 — Crashed Slaver Ship
Scavengers explore the ruins of the Crashed Slaver Ship for anything of value.

Knightly Order jobs

Available only with the Toxoids DLC enabled.

Empires with the Knights of the Toxic God origin use unique jobs on their starting habitat and consecrated habitats, as well as from upgraded homeworld features.

Order HQ capitals add only 1 Lord Commander job, but it has the equivalent output and upkeep of 100 workforce.

Job Cat. Stratum Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Sources Weight
Lord Commander Elite
  • +6 Naval capacity
  • +500 Amenities
  • Based on Quest event choices:
    • +2 / 4 / 6 / 8 / 10 Unity
    • +1 / 2 / 3 / 4 Research
  • Continued the Quest and has Cosmogenesis ascension perk:
    • +1 / 2 / 3 / 4 Advanced Logic (uses research multiplier)
 −2 Alloys
  • Order's headquarters habitat:
  • Habitat Central Control
 Base 15
  • × pop output
Knight Specialist
  • +4 Naval capacity
  • +1 Planetary defense armies
  • Based on Quest event choices:
    • +1 / 2 / 3 / 4 Unity
    • +1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 Research
  • Continued the Quest and has Cosmogenesis ascension perk:
    • +0.1 / 1 / 1.5 / 2 / 2.5 Advanced Logic (uses research multiplier)
  • Added by Quest event choices:
    • +500 Amenities
  • −1 Alloys
  • Upkeep may be removed with a sixth quest choice
  • Order's Keep
  • Order's Castle
  • Upgraded homeworld features
 Base: 24
  • × pop output
Squire Worker
  • +200 Amenities
  • +0.5 Planetary defense armies
  • +2.5% Job efficiency for Knights
  • +1.25% Job efficiency for Lord Commanders
  • +0.5% Upkeep for Knights and Lord Commanders
  • Lover's Pox planet modifier:
    • +0.1 Organic pop assembly
  • −0.5 Alloys
  • Lover's Pox planet modifier:
    • −0.5 Alloys (-1 total)
  • Order's Keep
  • Order's Castle
  • Order's Demesne
  • Upgraded homeworld features
 Base: 1.2

Wrangler jobs

Available only with the Grand Archive DLC enabled.

Empires with the Primal Calling origin use unique Wrangler jobs, with different variants depending on stance (or Warrior Culture civic). They are added by unique Wildlife buildings and the Untamed Wilds modifier. All these jobs belong to the Culture Worker category.

Job Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Stance
Wrangler
  • +1 Society
  • +1 Unity
  • +400 Amenities
  • −2 Consumer goods
  • Wrangler
Rancher
  • +1 Society
  • +1 Unity
  • +400 Amenities
  • −2 Consumer goods
  • Rancher
Trophy Hunter
  • +3 Food
  • +1 Unity
  • +600 Amenities
  • −2 Consumer goods
  • Trophy Hunter
Animal Handler
  • +2 Society
  • +2 Unity
  • −2 Consumer goods
  • Animal Handler
Beastfighter
  • +1 Society
  • +2 Unity
  • +600 Amenities
  • −1 Alloys
  • Warrior Culture (any stance)
Wrangler Drone
  • +1 Society
  • +1 Unity
  • +400 Amenities
  • −4 Minerals
  • Wild Consciousness
Symbiosis Integrator
  • +1.5 Society
  • +1.5 Unity
  • −4 Minerals
  • Symbiotic Partnership
Hunter Drone
  • +5 Food
  • +1 Unity
  • +400 Amenities
  • −4 Minerals
  • Hunter-Killers

Wilderness jobs

Empires with the Wilderness origin have unique names for most jobs, but additionally they have unique buildings that give exclusive refinery jobs. These produce strategic resources using minerals upkeep.

Job Cat. Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Primary source Weight
Motesymbiont
  • +2 Volatile motes
  • −10 Minerals
 Chemical Digesters Base: 10
Modifiers:
  • × pop output
  • ×7 if already employed in this job
Gaseosymbionte
  • +2 Exotic gases
 Exotic Gas Filters Base: 10
Modifiers:
  • × pop output
  • ×7 if already employed in this job
Crystalsymbiont
  • +2 Rare crystals
 Crystal Bed Base: 10
Modifiers:
  • × pop output
  • ×7 if already employed in this job

Overlord jobs

Overlord jobs are generated by Overlord holdings and can be used by both individualist and Gestalt Consciousness subjects. The upkeep is paid by the subject. All overlord jobs have a priority of 4 and the subject cannot lower or raise the job's priority.

Job Stratum Overlord output per 100 workforce Subject output per 100 workforce Subject upkeep per 100 workforce Source
Aid Worker +2.5 Unity +500 Amenities — Aid Agency
Overlord Trader
  • subject: +7 Trade
  • subject: +7 Energy
 +1000 Amenities
  • subject: −7 Trade
  • subject: −7 Energy
 Emporium
Empathic Marketer —
  • +6 Trade
  • If subject has breached the Shroud:
    • +2 Unity
  • −1 Consumer goods
  • If subject has breached the Shroud:
    • −1 Consumer goods (-2 total)
 Psionic Offices
Overlord Academic +1.5 Research +0.5 Research
  • subject: −2 Consumer goods
  • subject: −6 Minerals
  • subject: −4 Energy
 Satellite Campus
Overlord Propagandist +0.15 Influence — −0.5 Unity Ministry of Truth
Overlord Metallurgist
  • +3 Alloys
    • subject: +1 Alloys (+4 total)
 —
  • −6 Minerals
    • subject: −2 Minerals (-8 total)
 Offworld Foundry
Overlord Catalytic Technician
  • +3 Alloys
    • subject: +1 Alloys (+4 total)
  • −9 Food
    • subject: −3 Food (-12 total)

 −2 Consumer goods Franchise Headquarters
Overlord Recruiter

 +1.5 Planetary defense armies
  • −5 Energy
  • −1 Unity
 Recruitment Office

Overlord Breeder

  • overlord: −6 Minerals

Event jobs

Event jobs are generated by either planetary features or planet modifiers.

Job Cat. Stratum Output per 100 workforce Upkeep per 100 workforce Sources
Subterranean Liaison Officer
  • +8 Trade
  • +300 Amenities
 −2 Consumer Goods
  • Trader job swap:
    • Subterranean Contact Zone on planet
Subterranean Contact Drone
  • +6 Trade
  • +500 Amenities
  • : −3 Minerals
  • : −3 Energy
  • Logistics Drone job swap:
    • Subterranean Contact Zone on planet

 Dimensional Portal Researcher
Dimensional Portal Drone
  • +7 Physics
Additional effects from Probe the Dimensional Portal outcome:
    • Dimension of Suffering outcome:
      • −3 Physics if finished Close the Portal special project (+4 total)
    • High-Energy Dimension outcome:
      • +3 Energy
    • Null Void outcome:
      • +0.1 Dark Matter
        • +0.4 Dark Matter if finished Probe the Void special project (+0.5 total)
    • Mirror Dimension outcome:
      • +200 / 300 / 600 / 1200 Amenities
  • : −3 Consumer Goods
  • : −6 Energy
  • : −8 Minerals
  • Dimension of Suffering outcome:
    • : −1 Consumer Goods (-4 total)
    • : −1.5 Energy (-7.5 total)
    • : −2 Minerals (-10 total)
  • Physicist job swap:
    • Portal Research Area on planet
    • Extradimensional Experimentation planet modifier

 Space-Time Anomaly Researcher

  • +4 Physics

  • : −3 Consumer Goods

  • : −8 Minerals

    • Space-Time Anomaly on planet
Vent Researcher

 — Expanded Stratospheric Vents

Munitions Decommissioning Unit
  • +9 Engineering

Dismantler
Drone Dismantler
  • +5 Engineering

  • : −6 Minerals

Operator
Operator Drone
  • Base production:
  • +5 Alloys (+2 to +7 total based on choices)
  • +6 Alloys (+3 to +8 total based on choices)
Potential modifiers from Hidden Vault choices:
    • Remembrance Hall:
      • +0.5 Unity if Subterranean
      • +0.5 Unity if
        Masterful Crafters
        Masterful Crafters
        Mastercraft Inc.
      • +1 Unity if both of the above
    • Earthquake:
      • −1 Alloys if used bulky contraption
      • Negative effect averted if either:
    • Insight:
      • −1 Alloys if blasted the doors ( only)
    • Foundry Heart:
      • +1 Society if either:
      • +1 Engineering if either:
    • Work Conditions:
      • −1 Alloys if regulated work time ( only)
      • −20% Happiness of employed pop if rotated operators ( only)
      • +20% Amenities usage of employed pop if rotated drones ( only)
      • Negative effect averted with
    • Recycling Center:
      • +1 Alloys if either:
    • More or Less?:
      • +1 Alloys if Remnants
      • +1 Alloys if positive outcome (34% chance)
      • No effect (66% chance)
  • Base upkeep:
  • −5 Minerals (0 to −9 total based on choices)
Potential modifiers from Hidden Vault choices:
    • Earthquake:
      • +2 Minerals upkeep if used wall pipes
      • Negative effect averted if either:
    • Work Conditions:
      • −1 Minerals upkeep if regulated work time ( only)
    • Recycling Center:
      • −2 Minerals upkeep if either:
      • −1 Minerals upkeep (34% chance)
      • No effect (66% chance)
    • More or Less?:
      • +1 Minerals upkeep if Remnants
      • −2 Minerals and +2 Food upkeep if either:
      • +2 Minerals upkeep if positive outcome (34% chance)
      • +1 Minerals upkeep if negative outcome (26% chance)
      • No effect (40% chance)
 Operational Manufactorium

Pre-FTL jobs

Pre-FTL civilizations use the same strata system as normal empires, but their jobs provide negligible output.

Standard pre-FTL jobs

These jobs are used by regular pre-FTL civilizations.

Job Stratum Output per 100 workforce Source Ages Description
Industrial jobs
Bureaucrat Elite
  • +300 Amenities
  • +1 Unity
 Regional Capitals
Many early industrialist and post-industrialist societies are governed by an ever-expanding class of civil servants.
Priest Specialist
  • +200 Amenities
  • +1 Unity
 Regional Capitals
Religious leaders who occupy various priestly offices.
Researcher Specialist
  • +1 Research
  • −1 Consumer goods
 Regional Capitals
Researchers employing scientific methodology to advance the rapidly expanding knowledge base of their civilization.
Soldier Specialist +3 Planetary defense armies Regional Capitals
Professional Soldiers, typically armed with early projectile weapons powered by exothermic combustion.
Entertainer Specialist +800 Amenities Regional Capitals
Celebrities and media personalities that provide entertainment and distraction from day-to-day life.
Technician Worker +1 Energy Primitive Power Plant
These technicians shuffle coal or maintain primitive nuclear generators.
Farmer Worker +3 Food Primitive Farms
These farmers work the land, typically with the help of primitive but effective farming equipment.
Laborer Worker
  • +1 Alloys
  • +2 Consumer goods
  • −4 Minerals
 Primitive Factory
These early industrial laborers toil away in factories, which can now be operated day and night thanks to recent advances in electricity.
Miner Worker +3 Minerals Primitive Mine
Dangerous and sometimes lethal work in the dark bowels of the planet.
Pre-industrial jobs
Feudal Noble Elite
  • +300 Amenities
  • +1 Unity
 Stone Palaces
The landed nobility rule from their stone castles and keeps.
Cleric Specialist
  • +200 Amenities
  • +0.5 Unity
 Stone Palaces
Spiritual leaders and preachers who conduct religious ceremonies and oversee all matters concerning faith.
Scholar Specialist +0.5 Research Stone Palaces
The learned of this society, often relying on oral tradition to pass knowledge between generations.
Warrior Specialist +3 Planetary defense armies Stone Palaces
Members of a primitive warrior class. These fighters rely on bladed weapons, spears or various blunt instruments to see them through combat.
Peasant Worker
  • +2 Food (if )
  • +2 Minerals (if )
 Generated for all unemployed pops
The peasants toil endlessly in the fields, so that those perceived as their betters might prosper.
Hunter-Gatherer Worker
  • +1 Food (if )
  • +1 Minerals (if )
  • +100 Amenities
 Generated for all unemployed pops Stone Age
These semi-nomadic primitives survive by foraging and hunting. There is evidence of early tool use.

Hive Mind pre-FTL jobs

These jobs are used by Hive Mind pre-FTL civilizations.

Job Stratum Produces Source Ages Description
Spawning Drone Complex Drone +800 Amenities
  • Continental Nexus
  • Stone Hive
 All except
Having each member of a society reproduce is vastly inefficient. These drones specialize in generating whatever new drones the Hive needs.
Maintenance Drone Menial Drone +200 Amenities Generated for all unemployed pops All except
If there is one universal truth in this universe, it's that everything breaks. These drones are made to fix whatever is broken.
Industrial jobs
Synapse Drone Complex Drone
  • +300 Amenities
  • +1 Unity
 Continental Nexus
Administrating and coordinating an entire country is no easier if you are a single individual. These drones ensure that every drone has an appropriate task.
Brain Drone Complex Drone +1 Research Continental Nexus
Drones employing the scientific method are capable of taking massive leaps in their consciousness knowledge base. Often too large.
Warrior Drone Complex Drone +3 Planetary defense armies Continental Nexus
Specialized for war, these hulking drones have multiple specialized appendages perfected for crushing their own species.
Tech-Drone Menial Drone +1 Energy Primitive Power Plant
Operating dirty energy generators is not a job any living being can do forever. Still, these drones are enhanced with superior lungs as well as inherent radioactive resistance, so they last longer than most.
Agri-Drone Menial Drone +3 Food Primitive Farms
Food production overseen by a continent spanning hive-mind is a marvel of logistics and agronomy, every section of the designated agricultural area is used to its fullest.
Factory Drone Menial Drone
  • +2 Alloys
  • −2 Minerals
 Primitive Factory
Drones that are more or less part of the very factory they work in. These specialized laborers assemble everything from weapons to vehicles at an astounding rate.
Mining Drone Menial Drone +3 Minerals Primitive Mine
Sacrificing safety for success, these drones delve deep beneath the earth to feed the ever-hungry engine that is society.
Pre-industrial jobs
Nascent Synapse Drone Complex Drone
  • +300 Amenities
  • +0.5 Unity
 Stone Hive
Many early industrialist and post-industrialist societies are governed by an ever-expanding class of civil servants.
Cerebellum Drone Complex Drone +0.5 Research Stone Hive
Analyzing and discovering solutions to new difficulties is the hallmark of any flourishing society - these drones focus on finding solutions for other drones' problems and avoiding future pitfalls.
Simple Warrior Drone Complex Drone +3 Planetary defense armies Stone Hive
Having yet to fully come to grips with genetic specialization, these drones are grown bulkier to protect their hive. They have yet to evolve any specialized weapon appendages.
Basic Agricultural Drone Menial Drone
  • +2 Food (if )
  • +2 Minerals (if )
 Generated for all unemployed pops
Simple and unspecialized drones work the fields, their consciousness aware that food can be grown, but the task has yet to be optimized.
Sustenance Drone Menial Drone
  • +1 Food (if )
  • +1 Minerals (if )
  • +1 Amenities
 Generated for all unemployed pops Stone Age
The difference between a successful hivemind and a starved one comes down to how successful their resource procurement is.

Unique pre-FTL jobs

These jobs are used by unique pre-FTL civilizations.

Job Stratum Produces Source Pre-FTL civilization Description
Scavenger Worker
  • +1 Food
  • +1 Minerals
  • +1 Consumer Goods
 Junkheap Pre-FTL Ketlings
They scurry amongst the scrapyards, collecting discarded machinery and ancient electronics for repurposing.
Content Terraformer Elite
  • +3 Unity
  • +0.5 Stability
  • +100 Amenities
 Solarpunk buildings Habinte Unified Worlds
Having achieved their utopia these world sculptors are glad to rest.

Fallen empire jobs

Fallen empires use a special set of Precursor stratum jobs that have no upkeep. By default, pops without any building-provided jobs will be Hedonists.

Job Output per 100 workforce Source Precursor Description
Overseer
  • +5 Stability
  • +1000 Amenities
 Ancient Palace
  • Holy Guardians
  • Gestalt Consciousness
The stalwart few who have rejected lives of luxury in favor of public service. It is up to them to oversee and guide the remnants of their ancient civilization.
Protector
  • −100 Crime
  • +2 Planetary defense armies
  • Ancient Palace
  • Ziggurat of Justice
  • Gestalt Consciousness
So that the majority can sleep peacefully and enjoy their lives of extravagance, these rough individuals stand ready in the night to visit violence on those who would do them harm.
Hedonist +1 Unity Generated for all unemployed pops
  • Holy Guardians
  • Gestalt Consciousness
A life of total opulence, where every possible material need or desire can be satisfied.
Archivist +5 Research Master Archive (found only on The Archives) Keepers of Knowledge
Each data fragment must be filed, cataloged and indexed exactly according to the ancient rites as they were established eons ago by the First Librarian.
Xeno-Keeper
  • +1000 Amenities
  • −20 Crime
  • +1 Planetary defense armies
 Xeno Preserve (found only on The Preserve) Enigmatic Observers
These precursor zookeepers look after the needs of the Xeno-Wards under their care.
Xeno-Ward +1 Unity Primitives stratum Enigmatic Observers
These specimens have been gathered here, in settings that approximate their natural environments, to ensure the preservation of their species in an often chaotic and destructive galaxy.
Holy Guardians
Sky Cardinal
  • +5 Stability
  • +1000 Amenities
 Ancient Palace
And it was there, in the sky, that the future was born. The light was blinding, but a few could see past it. They took it upon themselves to lead others forwards.
Augur of the Shroud +5 Unity Phased Empyrean Shrine (found only on Sky Temple)
It speaks, but not in words. To divine any kind of meaning, one must hover on the precipice of madness.
Acolyte of the Hyperspanner +6 Energy
  • Class-3 / Class-4 Singularity
  • Replaces Technicians
Remember also the hyperspanner, and how it was used by Bazagg the Shining to tame the sun when it seemed like darkness would overtake the world.
Acolyte of the Hammer +6 Minerals
  • Quantum Drilling Plant / Hub
  • Replaces Miners
And with one mighty blow, Saint Mazakudal used her burning hammer to split the skull of the First Wraith in two, opening up the path to the crystals.
Acolyte of the Plow +8 Food
  • Nourishment Complex / Center
  • Replaces Farmers
For was it not the plow, this most noble of instruments, that Kazargiel used to sever the poisonous tail of the thrice-cursed demon and stave off famine?
Acolyte of the Workshop
  • +300 Amenities
  • +6 Consumer goods
 Generated for all unemployed pops
And so Aznakha said to the acolytes, "go to the workshops, and form with your own crafts the future you seek."
Ancient Caretakers
Guardian
  • −25 Crime
  • +2 Planetary defense armies
 Ancient Control Center
These ancient machines maintain a ceaseless vigil over the long-expired remains of billions of cryogenically frozen sapients.
Caretaker +500 Amenities Generated for all unemployed pops
These ancient machines tirelessly maintain and repair the decaying machinery of the ringworld on which they reside.

References

  1. ↑ BUG: This purge type is not working properly.
Game concepts
Exploration Exploration • FTL • Unique systems • L-Cluster • Pre-FTL species • Fallen empire • Spaceborne aliens • Enclaves • Guardians • Marauders • Caravaneers
Celestial bodies Celestial body • Colonization • Planetary features • Planet modifiers
Discovery Discovery • Anomaly • Archaeological site • Astral rift • Relics • Collection
Species Species • Pop modification • Biological traits • Machine traits • Population • Species rights • Ethics
Leaders Leader • Common leader traits • Commander traits • Official traits • Scientist traits • Paragons
Governance Empire • Origin • Government • Civics • Council • Policies • Edicts • Factions • Traditions • Ascension perks • Ambition • Situations
Economy Resources • Planetary management • Districts • Jobs • Designation • Trade • Megastructures • Waystation
Buildings Planet capital • Common buildings • Planet unique • Empire unique • Holdings
Ships Ship • Arkship • Ship designer • Core components • Weapon components • Utility components • Mutations • Offensive mutations
